mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals rapid labor market tightening, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and drives positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment drop, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating higher corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than half of its occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when added to a single-proxy baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, a sustained decline in the industrial production index indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during rate hikes consistently coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 3.0 percent triggers algorithmic momentum buying and fundamental re-rating, producing positive S&P 500 returns within the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 generates negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances following GDP prints above 3.0%, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a federal funds rate below 0.5 percent creates a negative real rate environment that forces institutional investors to rotate into equities, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 50% of observations, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for historical volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates underlying economic resilience, supporting corporate revenue trajectories and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in months with payroll growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail and food services sales exceeding 1.5 percent signals immediate consumer demand contraction, prompting earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the sales drop, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
